summary: Small IF subsystem matching slice. The loaded changed-file metadata and raw diff show a one-line change in src/melee/if/soundtest.c, zero-initializing the global struct object un_803FA258. The PR body describes this as a split from #2617 scoped to src/melee/if, and decomp-dev reported one new match in main/melee/if/soundtest plus four unmatched-item improvements. Review comments on src/melee/if/textlib.c objected to replacing existing semantic variable names with address-style names/defines, but the loaded changed-files list does not include textlib.c, so those comments are retained as review evidence with slice inconsistency noted.

key_files.role: Only file in loaded changed-files metadata and raw diff; changed un_803FA258 declaration to include a zero initializer.
Mentioned in PR body file list and review comments; reviewer objected to variable renames/define aliasing in this file, but it is absent from loaded changed-files metadata and raw diff.

what_changed.text: In src/melee/if/soundtest.c, the declaration of un_803FA258 changed from an uninitialized global struct object to an explicitly zero-initialized object: `} un_803FA258;` became `} un_803FA258 = { 0 };`.

decomp-dev reported GALE01 progress for the PR head: matched code +188 bytes, one new match for `main/melee/if/soundtest` item `un_80301BA8`, and four improvements involving `un_802FFF2C`, `.data`, `un_80300AF4`, and `un_80300B58`.
The PR was presented as an isolated directory/subsystem split from #2617 for `src/melee/if`, created from origin/master with `git diff --check origin/master...HEAD` passing for the slice.

smart_moves.text: Splitting a larger matching branch by directory/subsystem made the IF changes easier to review.
The PR body distinguished preliminary full-branch scores from authoritative per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting, reducing overclaiming from the unsplit source branch.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching slice only changes initialization or data shape, keep match claims tied to the per-PR report and the exact affected unit rather than broad source-branch totals.
Address-style renames should not replace existing clearer variable names through defines or aliasing unless there is specific evidence supporting the rename.

naming_conventions.text: Reviewer feedback favored preserving existing names such as `devtext_drawlist`, `devtext_poolhead`, `white`, `red`, `green`, and `blue` over replacing them with address-style globals like `un_804D6E18` or `un_804DDC88` and defines.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The concrete code tactic visible in the diff was adding an explicit `{ 0 }` initializer to a global struct object in soundtest.c, which coincided with reported soundtest text/data matching progress.
The PR body documents use of an isolated path slice and `git diff --check` as local review hygiene for a directory split.

notes: Loaded changed-files metadata and raw diff include only src/melee/if/soundtest.c with one added and one deleted line.

The PR body lists both src/melee/if/soundtest.c and src/melee/if/textlib.c, and review comments reference textlib.c hunks, but loaded changed-files metadata does not include textlib.c. This intake does not infer the final textlib.c diff beyond the review excerpts.
The PR body includes preliminary full unsplit branch scores; the decomp-dev bot comment is stronger evidence for this PR slice's reported result.
No build logs, objdiff details, final source files, or merge-resolution diff beyond the one raw diff excerpt are loaded.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid define-backed variable renames
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2623, review feedback on src/melee/if/textlib.c explicitly objected to renaming existing variables and using defines in their place. Candidate lesson: do not replace existing clearer globals or color names with address-style aliases/defines unless evidence supports a real canonical rename.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Tie split-PR match claims to per-PR reports
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2623 was split from a larger branch and its body warned that per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative for the slice. Candidate lesson: when intaking or reviewing split matching PRs, distinguish preliminary full-branch scores from the slice-specific report.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not hide speculative global renames behind defines
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer feedback in PR #2623 says to avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place. This supports the existing standard against define alias global renames and may be useful as an example under `no-define-alias-global-renames` or `conservative-naming`.
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence is direct reviewer feedback, but it should be curated as an example or reinforcement rather than promoted automatically because the full final textlib.c diff is not loaded.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a general rule that `{ 0 }` initializers should be added to globals for matching; this PR only shows one soundtest.c case and lacks objdiff/codegen reasoning.
Do not claim textlib.c was part of the final changed-files set; loaded changed-files metadata and raw diff only show soundtest.c despite PR body and review comments referencing textlib.c.
Do not treat the preliminary full-branch matched-code/data percentages as final PR #2623 validation; the PR body itself says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative.
